title: daucus carota subsp. dentatus
intro: Daucus carota subsp. dentatus, commonly known as the wild carrot or dentate carrot, is a subspecies of the carrot plant that grows in the wild. It is a biennial herb with a long history of use in herbal medicine, valued for its carotenoids, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of Daucus carota subsp. dentatus include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which are thought to contribute to its health benefits, such as supporting immune function, reducing inflammation, and protecting against cell damage. Daucus carota subsp. dentatus can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
